The station is owned by Blueberry Broadcasting, LLC and simulcasts co-owned [WTOS-FM](/wiki/WTOS-FM "WTOS-FM").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

For the radio station in Madison, Wisconsin, licensed as WTUX from 2001 to 2009, see [WHIT](/wiki/WHIT "WHIT").

Radio station in Gouldsboro, Maine, United States

**WTUX** (101.1 [FM](/wiki/FM_broadcasting "FM broadcasting")) is an American [mainstream rock](/wiki/Mainstream_rock "Mainstream rock") radio station licensed to [Gouldsboro, Maine](/wiki/Gouldsboro,_Maine "Gouldsboro, Maine"), United States. The station is owned by Blueberry Broadcasting, LLC and simulcasts co-owned [WTOS-FM](/wiki/WTOS-FM "WTOS-FM").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=WTUX&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

This station received its original [construction permit](/wiki/Construction_permit "Construction permit") from the [Federal Communications Commission](/wiki/Federal_Communications_Commission "Federal Communications Commission") (FCC) on May 25, [2006](/wiki/2006_in_radio "2006 in radio").[[4]](#cite_note-fccp-4) The new station was assigned the WLEK [call sign](/wiki/Call_sign "Call sign") by the FCC on April 30, 2009.[[5]](#cite_note-fcc1-5)

In April 2008, permit holder Louis Vitali filed an application with the FCC to transfer the permit to a company he controlled, Blueberry Broadcasting, LLC. The transfer was approved by the FCC on April 30, 2008, and the transaction was consummated on June 4, 2008.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

On January 7, 2009, WLEK applied to change its [community of license](/wiki/Community_of_license "Community of license") to [Gouldsboro, Maine](/wiki/Gouldsboro,_Maine "Gouldsboro, Maine").[[7]](#cite_note-cp2-7) As of May 20, 2009[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=WTUX&action=edit), the FCC had accepted this application for filing but had taken no further action on the request.[[7]](#cite_note-cp2-7) WLEK received its [license to cover](/wiki/Broadcast_license "Broadcast license") with [Machias](/wiki/Machias,_Maine "Machias, Maine") as its community of license from the FCC on May 13, 2009.[[8]](#cite_note-flic-8)

WLEK changed its call letters to WTUX on May 20, 2010,[[5]](#cite_note-fcc1-5) and in June 2010 signed on the air, simulcasting Augusta-based [WTOS-FM](/wiki/WTOS-FM "WTOS-FM").
